title: coreopsis fasciculata
intro: Coreopsis fasciculata, also known as the golden tickseed, is a flowering plant species native to North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may help to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and combat infections. The health benefits of Coreopsis fasciculata include its potential to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve skin health, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Coreopsis fasciculata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for convenient and targeted use of its medicinal properties.
